title: Create a dependency when creating multiple subitems on automations
excerpt:
Is it possible to create a dependencie when creating multiple subitems on automations? 
I know you can assign a dependency on an Item already created on the board. is there a way to do it with the items you are creating …

posts.author: rramirezz (Roberto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it possible to create a dependencie when creating multiple subitems on automations?

I know you can assign a dependency on an Item already created on the board. is there a way to do it with the items you are creating on the automation?

Example of automation: When an item is created create a sub item (a) and create a sub item (b) and create a sub item (c).

Is it possible to create a dependency for sub item (b) on sub item (a), and a dependency for sub item (c) on sub item (b)?

posts.author: Alastair_Foggo (Alastair Foggo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi. I’m trying to do exactly the same thing.
When I create the dependencies manually on a board, the changes to dates works fine. When I create the dependencies by automation Adjust the subitem timeline of an item to refelct the changes made in the date of its subitem dependent on item, it fails.
I’ve been in touch with monday.com and the developers are having a look at it for me.

posts.author: marcelo.leitao (Marcelo Leitão da Silveira)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @rramirezz , did you get any answers for that? I’m almost killing myself trying to solve this issue!!!

posts.author: Os-BZ (osnat)
posts.cookedHtml: Facing the exact same issue, did you mange to solve it?

posts.author: aknight (Austin Knight)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in need of the very same thing. Any response yet? Is monday looking to develop this?

posts.author: alex.walton (Alex Walton)
posts.cookedHtml: I could use this. I have been asking Monday support as well as 3rd party app developers and have not found a workable solution yet.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all,

We have several apps in the marketplace and we’ve started exploring a way to build an app that would solve this. I’d love to talk to any of you that are facing this challenge to better understand the use case and what exactly you’re trying to achieve so we can design the right solution.

If any of you are up for a quick discussion, please shoot me an email to daniel@fantasymedia.io.

Thanks!

posts.author: snaguib3 (Sara Naguib)
posts.cookedHtml:
what is the name of the app?
i m trying to do the same thing.
i have a timeline for subitems and i m trying to link it to the timeline in the item but i cant find a way to do that

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @snaguib3

We developed an app that solves this exact pain point of linking between item’s timeline and subitems’ timeline. There’s a few options -

When subitem is created you can set the same timeline as the parent item’s timeline -

Screenshot 2024-01-14 at 12.04.002352×454 30.6 KB

When the parent item changes you can change the subitem’s timeline + add some offset to it -

Screenshot 2024-01-14 at 12.06.121984×370 40.6 KB

You can also go the other way and rollup all subitem’s timeline to the parent item -

Screenshot 2024-01-14 at 12.07.102200×390 30.8 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Our Subitems Pro Automations app introduces numerous automations to help manage dependencies between subitems as well as subitems and parent item. Here are just a few examples:

  1. Automatically set dependencies between subitems

    image900×352 24.7 KB

  2. Set each subitem date column value to parent date with customized offset

    image1290×308 18 KB

  3. Similar to above, but offset is calculated individually for each subitem based on value saved in subitem number column

    image1311×336 18.3 KB

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!
